question: A train travels 180 km uphill at 45 km/h and returns downhill at 60 km/h along the same route. What is the average speed for the entire trip in km/h? Round to 2 decimal places.
A shopkeeper buys 120 items for $18 each. He sells 70% of them at $25 each and the rest at a 10% loss. What is the total profit?

Pipe A fills a tank in 8 hours, Pipe B fills it in 12 hours, and Pipe C empties it in 18 hours. If all three pipes are opened together, how long does it take to fill the tank? Round to 2 decimal places.
A sum of $8000 is invested, part at 6% simple interest and the rest at 10% simple interest. After 4 years, the total interest earned is $2560. How much was invested at 10%?
A bacteria colony doubles every 5 hours. Starting with 150 bacteria, after how many hours will the population first exceed 19,000?
Solve for x: x squared minus 11x plus 24 equals 0. Give the larger root only.
If log base 3 of (x minus 1) equals 4, what is x?
Solve for x: square root of (2x + 3) plus square root of (x - 1) equals 5.
A quadratic equation has roots x = 4 and x = -7. What is the coefficient of x in the standard form x squared + bx + c = 0?
If f(x) = 2x squared - 5x + 1, what is f(4)?
A circle has radius 9 cm. A sector of 120 degrees is removed. What is the area of the remaining part of the circle? Use pi = 3.14159 and round to 2 decimal places.
A rectangle is inscribed inside a circle of radius 13 cm. One side of the rectangle is 10 cm. What is the area of the rectangle?
A ladder 25 metres long leans against a wall. The foot of the ladder is 7 metres from the wall. What height does the ladder reach on the wall? Round to 2 decimal places.
A sphere has radius 6 m. What is the volume of the sphere? Use pi = 3.14159 and round to 2 decimal places. Write your final answer as: Answer: [number]
A cube has side length 10 cm. A sphere of radius 5 cm is perfectly inscribed inside it. What is the volume of the part of the cube outside the sphere? Use pi = 3.14159 and round to 2 decimal places.
A projectile is launched vertically upward with initial velocity 49 m/s. Using g = 9.8 m/s squared, after how many seconds does it return to the ground?
A car accelerates uniformly from 10 m/s to 40 m/s in 6 seconds. What distance does it travel during this time? Write your final answer as: Answer: [number]
A force of 120 N acts on an object causing an acceleration of 3 m/s squared. What is the mass of the object in kilograms?
A 5 kg object moving at 12 m/s collides elastically with a stationary object. What was the initial kinetic energy of the moving object? Write your final answer as: Answer: [number]
An object is dropped from a height of 245 metres. Using g = 9.8 m/s squared and ignoring air resistance, how many seconds does it take to reach the ground? Round to 2 decimal places.
Find the sum of the first 30 terms of the arithmetic sequence: 5, 9, 13, 17, ...
What is the 20th term of the arithmetic sequence that starts at 7 with common difference 5?
Find the sum of the first 7 terms of the geometric sequence: 3, 12, 48, 192, ...
A geometric sequence has first term 2 and common ratio 5. What is the 8th term?
A train travels 240 km at 80 km/h, then 360 km at 120 km/h, and finally 150 km at 50 km/h. What is the average speed for the entire journey in km/h? Round to 2 decimal places.
Solve for x: (3x - 7)(2x + 5) = 4x squared + 19x - 15. Give the larger root only.
A cylinder has radius 7 cm and height 13 cm. What is its total surface area? Use pi = 3.14159 and round to 2 decimal places.
A car accelerates uniformly from 12 m/s to 38 m/s in 7 seconds, then continues at constant speed for 15 seconds. What total distance does it travel in metres? Round to 2 decimal places.
Find the sum of the first 15 terms of the geometric sequence: 5, 15, 45, 135, ...
What is the sum of all even integers from 2 to 200 inclusive?
A recursive sequence is defined by a1 = 3 and a(n+1) = 2a(n) + 1. What is a6?
A ball is thrown upward from a height of 20 metres with initial velocity 24 m/s. Using g = 9.8 m/s squared, what maximum height does it reach above the ground? Round to 2 decimal places.
Two cyclists start 210 km apart and ride toward each other. One travels at 24 km/h and the other at 18 km/h. After how many hours will they meet?
